steps.title: Recognize that both equations describe the same line
steps.content: Multiply the first equation by 6:$$6(5x+7y)=6(8)\Rightarrow 30x+42y=48$$This is exactly the second equation. So the "system" is really one line: $5x+7y=8$. Any point on that line satisfies both equations.

steps.title: Test the parametrized choices in Desmos
steps.content: Define $L(x,y)=5x+7y$. A point is on the solution line when $L(x,y)=8$. Set $r=[0,1]$ and evaluate each choice; the correct parametrization returns $[8,8]$, so it works for more than one value of $r$ and represents the whole line.

steps.title: Check each choice against $5x+7y=8$
steps.content:
• A) $\left(r,\ -\frac{5r}{7}+\frac{8}{7}\right)$: $5r+7\left(-\frac{5r}{7}+\frac{8}{7}\right)=5r-5r+8=8$. Works for all $r$. ✓
  • B) $\left(-\frac{5r}{7}+\frac{8}{7},\ r\right)$: $5\left(-\frac{5r}{7}+\frac{8}{7}\right)+7r=-\frac{25r}{7}+\frac{40}{7}+7r=\frac{24r+40}{7}$, which only equals 8 when $r=\frac{2}{3}$. ✗
  • C) $\left(-\frac{5r}{7}+8,\ \frac{5r}{7}+48\right)$: $5\left(-\frac{5r}{7}+8\right)+7\left(\frac{5r}{7}+48\right)=-\frac{25r}{7}+40+5r+336=\frac{10r}{7}+376\ne 8$. ✗
  • D) $\left(\frac{r}{6}+8,\ -\frac{r}{6}+48\right)$: $5\left(\frac{r}{6}+8\right)+7\left(-\frac{r}{6}+48\right)=\frac{5r}{6}+40-\frac{7r}{6}+336=-\frac{r}{3}+376\ne 8$. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
